nodes.context: Italian-American Mafia crime family based in Pueblo and Denver, Colorado, active from c. 1920s to 2000s.
Bootlegging organization headed by Pete and Sam Carlino, operating from 1922 to 1931 in Colorado.
Italian-American Mafia; the Denver branch evolved from Roma's organization into the Smaldone crime family.
Crime family based in St. Louis; partnered with Colorado family in skimming profits from Riviera hotel and casino.
New York-based Mafia family listed as an ally in the infobox.
Chicago-based Mafia organization listed as an ally in the infobox.
Kansas City-based Mafia family listed as an ally in the infobox.
Los Angeles-based Mafia family listed as an ally in the infobox.
New Orleans-based Mafia family listed as an ally in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The family originated as a bootlegging organization headed by the brothers Pete and Sam Carlino
edges.context: The Colorado crime family originated from the Carlino brothers' bootlegging organization.

edges.evidence_quote: Roma's organization evolved into the Denver branch of La Cosa Nostra, which became known as the Smaldone crime family.
edges.context: The Colorado crime family is the Denver branch of La Cosa Nostra.

edges.evidence_quote: Beginning in 1973 or earlier, the Colorado family partnered with the St. Louis crime family in skimming profits from the Riviera hotel and casino in Las Vegas.
edges.context: The Colorado family partnered with the St. Louis crime family in skimming casino profits.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Chicago Outfit, Kansas City crime family, Los Angeles crime family, New Orleans crime family, St. Louis crime family
edges.context: The Bonanno crime family is listed as an ally of the Colorado crime family.

edges.context: The Chicago Outfit is listed as an ally of the Colorado crime family.
